domain_name: Satellite Remote Sensing and ML for Water/Land Feature Mapping

background_concepts.concept: Multispectral bands and spectral indices (e.g., NDVI/NDWI)
background_concepts.why_needed: Irrigation and water-feature mapping readings assume familiarity with the reflectance bands and derived indices used to distinguish vegetation, water, and soil.

suggested_resources.title: Echoes in Space / Introduction to Remote Sensing (EO College materials referenced in EO4GEO)

background_concepts.why_needed: Cascade detection and weakly-supervised detection readings assume the reader knows the distinction between pixel-level segmentation and bounding-box detection.

suggested_resources.title: Satellite Imagery Feature Detection using Deep CNN (background on segmentation/detection)

domain_name: Precipitation, Weather, and Climate Forecasting via Deep Learning

background_concepts.why_needed: The GNN and stacking-ensemble readings assume understanding of ensemble weather prediction (multiple perturbed forecasts) without explaining it.

suggested_resources.title: GFDL / NOAA guides on climate & weather modeling

background_concepts.why_needed: DiffObs applies generative diffusion to satellite forecasting but assumes the forward/reverse noising framework is understood.

suggested_resources.title: Denoising Diffusion-based Generative Modeling: Foundations and Applications (CVPR 2022 tutorial)
Introduction to Diffusion Models for Machine Learning

domain_name: Flood and Drought Risk Modeling and Impact Estimation

background_concepts.why_needed: The susceptibility-mapping reading assumes knowledge of how conditioning factors combine into hazard maps.

suggested_resources.title: UN-SPIDER Recommended Practice: Flood Mapping

domain_name: AI for Climate Policy and Ecosystem Valuation

background_concepts.concept: Integrated Assessment Models (IAMs) and Social Cost of Carbon
background_concepts.why_needed: ML-IAM and climate-policy readings assume the reader knows what IAMs are and how they couple climate and economic systems to compute policy costs.

suggested_resources.title: Introduction to integrated assessment modeling of climate change (ScienceDirect chapter overview)
Inside the Integrated Assessment Models: Four Issues in Climate Economics (SEI)

domain_name: AI Flood Mapping and Forecasting from Satellite Data

background_concepts.concept: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) backscatter and water detection
background_concepts.why_needed: Flood-mapping readings rely on SAR imaging (all-weather, specular reflection off water yielding dark pixels) without explaining the physics.

suggested_resources.title: Flood mapping with Synthetic Aperture Radar (EO4GEO/FSU-Jena course)
UN-SPIDER Step-by-step: Radar-based flood mapping with Python

domain_name: Streamflow and Hydrological Modeling with ML

background_concepts.why_needed: Streamflow-prediction readings assume understanding of the hydrologic cycle, catchments, and how precipitation becomes discharge.

suggested_resources.title: USGS Precipitation-Runoff Modeling System (PRMS)
Rainfall-Runoff Modeling overview (ScienceDirect Topics)

domain_name: Remote Sensing Foundation Models and Segmentation Methods

background_concepts.concept: Foundation models / pretraining and transfer learning
background_concepts.why_needed: These readings assume familiarity with the pretrain-then-finetune paradigm and why it helps data-scarce EO tasks.

suggested_resources.title: Introduction to Earth Foundation Models (IGARSS 2025 tutorial)
Introducing Geospatial Foundation Models in ArcGIS (Esri)

background_concepts.concept: Semantic segmentation and the U-Net architecture
background_concepts.why_needed: Segmentation and change-detection readings assume encoder-decoder segmentation networks and pixel-level labeling are understood.

suggested_resources.title: Satellite imagery segmentation using U-Net (tutorial)

domain_name: Tropical Cyclone and Extreme Storm Prediction

background_concepts.why_needed: TC readings assume the conventional satellite-based intensity-estimation framework and TC structure (eye, CDO) as background.

suggested_resources.title: The Dvorak Technique Explained (NOAA OSPO)

background_concepts.why_needed: The typhoon-variable estimation reading uses conditional denoising diffusion but doesn't teach the underlying generative framework.

suggested_resources.title: Denoising Diffusion-based Generative Modeling (CVPR 2022 tutorial)

background_concepts.why_needed: Coastline-forecasting readings assume knowledge of how shorelines are extracted from optical imagery (water/land segmentation).

suggested_resources.title: Satellite image segmentation using U-Net for shoreline delineation (overview)

background_concepts.concept: Statistical vs. dynamical downscaling and GCMs
background_concepts.why_needed: Downscaling readings assume understanding of coarse-resolution GCM outputs and the predictor-predictand statistical relationship.

suggested_resources.title: Climate Model Downscaling (NOAA GFDL)
Statistical downscaling key term (Fiveable Intro to Climate Science)

domain_name: Compound Climate Extremes and Disaster Hazard Prediction

background_concepts.why_needed: The concurrence reading assumes the notion of co-occurring hazards (heatwave-drought-wildfire) as an established climate-science concept.

suggested_resources.title: GFDL climate modeling / extremes overview

background_concepts.why_needed: The S2S extremes reading assumes familiarity with the forecast horizon between weather and climate timescales.

suggested_resources.title: Fiveable Intro to Climate Science (downscaling/prediction terms)

domain_name: Weather and Climate Model Emulation and Downscaling

background_concepts.concept: Bias correction and super-resolution of meteorological fields
background_concepts.why_needed: The downscaling benchmark assumes understanding of coarse-to-fine mapping and bias correction of model outputs.

suggested_resources.title: Climate Model Downscaling (EPRI Climate Data User Guide)

domain_name: Satellite Land Cover and Water Body Segmentation

background_concepts.concept: Semantic segmentation metrics (IoU, Dice) and class imbalance
background_concepts.why_needed: The adaptive Dice loss and LCLU readings assume the reader knows segmentation loss functions and evaluation metrics like IoU.

suggested_resources.title: Satellite imagery segmentation using U-NET (covers Dice loss, Jaccard/IoU)
